rationale: The determinism predicate requires proving Vec<u8> spec-level equality (r1->Some_0 == r2->Some_0) given only view equality (e1@ == r1->Some_0@ and e1@ == r2->Some_0@). Verus encodes Vec<u8> as an uninterpreted SMT sort with no axiom relating spec-level == to @ (view). Confirmed via standalone test: assert(v1 == v2) under requires(v1@ == v2@) fails. Searched vstd/std_specs/vec.rs and view.rs for axioms (axiom_spec_len, axiom_vec_index_decreases, vec_clone_deep_view_proof, axiom_spec_into_iter, axiom_vec_has_resolved, axiom_vec_decreases_to_view, lemma_vec_obeys_view_eq, etc.) -- none relate spec-level Vec == to view. Tried =~= and =~~= extensional equality -- Vec is not marked #[verifier::ext_equal]. The case-split / view-equality chain closes successfully up to assert(r1->Some_0@ =~= r2->Some_0@); only the final Vec equality is unprovable. Given policy opaque_ok=False, this appears to be a generator issue (Vec equality should have been decomposed into len + element-wise checks).
